content.problemStatement:
Chef has two integers AAA and BBB.

Chef wants to find the **minimum** value of lcm(A,X)−gcd(B,X)\texttt{lcm}(A, X) - \texttt{gcd}(B, X)lcm(A,X)−gcd(B,X) where XXX is any positive integer.

Help him determine this value.

Note: gcd(P,Q)\texttt{gcd}(P, Q)gcd(P,Q) denotes the greatest common divisor of PPP and QQQ and lcm(P,Q)\texttt{lcm}(P, Q)lcm(P,Q) denotes the least common multiple of PPP and QQQ.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers AAA and BBB — the integers mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum value of lcm(A,X)−gcd(B,X)\texttt{lcm}(A, X) - \texttt{gcd}(B, X)lcm(A,X)−gcd(B,X).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible value of lcm(A, X) − gcd(B, X) over all positive integers X.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case the answer equals A minus gcd(A, B):
- gcd(12,15)=3 → 12‑3=9
- gcd(5,50)=5 → 5‑5=0
- gcd(9,11)=1 → 9‑1=8

solution.approach: For any X let g = gcd(A, X). Then lcm(A, X) = A * (X / g). Write X = g·k where gcd(A/g, k) = 1, so lcm(A, X) = A·k. The expression becomes A·k − gcd(B, g·k). Since gcd(B, g·k) ≤ g·k, we have A·k − gcd(B, g·k) ≥ A·k − g·k = k·(A − g) ≥ A − g. The lower bound A − g is achieved when k = 1 (i.e., X = g) and when g also divides B, because then gcd(B, g) = g. The largest possible g that divides both A and B is d = gcd(A, B). Choosing X = d gives lcm(A, d) = A and gcd(B, d) = d, so the value equals A − d. No smaller value is possible, therefore the answer for each test case is A − gcd(A, B). The algorithm is simply to compute the greatest common divisor of A and B and subtract it from A.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T · log min(A, B)) – each gcd is computed with the Euclidean algorithm.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(A - math.gcd(A, B)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
